user_message: Just got this from Sandra Taylor at Northwind Traders -- I need help figuring out pipeline hygiene. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-02-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Northwind Traders)

[00:01:15] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is pipeline hygiene.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:20:00] David (Northwind Traders): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:29:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:34:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-O trap (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-O failure mode (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-O -- Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
